subtasks.description: Query MongoDB contracts collection to retrieve all active billing contracts with contracted annual value and key contract metadata
Query SQL invoicing warehouse to retrieve all invoices issued in 2024, grouped by contract ID with total amount invoiced per contract

Reconcile active contracts (MongoDB) with 2024 actual invoices (SQL), calculate variance percentage for each contract, and flag contracts with material variance exceeding 10% threshold. Include reconciliation logic, variance calculation, and classification of flagged contracts

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks required: (1) Retrieve active contracts from MongoDB (mongo_query, small tier—straightforward collection query with no complex aggregation), (2) Retrieve 2024 invoice totals from SQL warehouse (sql_gen, small tier—single table aggregate with simple date filtering), (3) Reconcile the two sources, calculate variance, and flag material deviations (cross_recon, mid tier—requires matching contracts across sources, computing percentage variance against a threshold, and classifying flagged records). The reconciliation is the only multi-source decision point and justifies mid-tier reasoning to handle variance logic reliably. Two upstream sources feed into the final reconciliation; mid tier ensures robust matching and variance classification without unnecessary frontier capability.

output.sql: SELECT invoice_facts.contract_id, SUM(invoice_facts.amount_usd) AS total_amount_invoiced, COUNT(invoice_facts.invoice_id) AS invoice_count FROM invoice_facts WHERE invoice_facts.invoice_date >= '2024-01-01' AND invoice_facts.invoice_date < '2025-01-01' GROUP BY invoice_facts.contract_id ORDER BY invoice_facts.contract_id

output.raw_output:
{
  "reconciliation_logic": {
    "primary_source": "MongoDB (active_contracts)",
    "secondary_source": "SQL (2024_invoices)",
    "join_type": "LEFT JOIN",
    "variance_threshold_pct": 10,
    "variance_calculation": "((sql_total_invoiced - mongo_annual_value) / mongo_annual_value) * 100",
    "classification_rules": {
      "material_variance_high": "variance > 10%",
      "material_variance_low": "variance < -10%",
      "within_tolerance": "-10% <= variance <= 10%",
      "no_invoices_found": "sql_value is null"
    }
  },
  "reconciled": [
    {
      "entity_key": "contract_id",
      "mongo_field": "annual_value_usd",
      "sql_field": "total_amount_invoiced",
      "join_condition": "mongo.contract_id = sql.contract_id",
      "variance_formula": "((sql.total_amount_invoiced - mongo.annual_value_usd) / mongo.annual_value_usd) * 100",
      "output_schema": {
        "contract_id": "string",
        "vendor_name": "string",
        "term_months": "number",
        "mongo_annual_value_usd": "number",
        "sql_total_invoiced_2024": "number|null",
        "sql_invoice_count": "number|null",
        "variance_usd": "number",
        "variance_pct": "number",
        "material_variance_flag": "boolean",
        "classification": "string"
      },
      "classification_mapping": {
        "material_variance_flag_true_positive": "overinvoiced - flag for review",
        "material_variance_flag_true_negative": "underinvoiced - flag for review",
        "within_tolerance": "stable - no action required",
        "no_recent_activity": "no invoices in 2024",
        "at_risk": "contract active but no billing activity",
        "expand": "invoiced > annual_value + 10%",
        "reduce": "invoiced < annual_value - 10%",
        "hold": "within tolerance range"
      }
    }
  ],
  "sample_output_records": [
    {
      "contract_id": "CNT-001",
      "vendor_name": "Vendor A",
      "term_months": 12,
      "mongo_annual_value_usd": 100000,
      "sql_total_invoiced_2024": 115000,
      "sql_invoice_count": 12,
      "variance_usd": 15000,
      "variance_pct": 15.0,
      "material_variance_flag": true,
      "classification": "overinvoiced"
    },
    {
      "contract_id": "CNT-002",
      "vendor_name": "Vendor B",
      "term_months": 24,
      "mongo_annual_value_usd": 250000,
      "sql_total_invoiced_2024": 245000,
